report.benchmark_prompt_text: Helix benchmarks how KV-cache compression changes prompt perplexity, token selection, GPU memory, and serialized session size during real inference on instruction-tuned models. The passage intentionally mixes technical nouns, verbs, numbers, and varied sentence lengths so the prompt distribution is not a trivial repetition loop. Local-first agents, compressed memory systems, retrieval traces, JSON tool outputs, and long-context summaries all appear in the text because the benchmark should resemble realistic product traffic rather than synthetic noise.
